description: Note **Not available in FLAC2D . The Columnar-Basalt (COMBA) model (Detournay et al 2016, Meng et al 2020) accounts for the presence of up to four arbitrary orientations of weakness (ubiquitous joint) in a non-isotropic elastic matrix. The model can be applied to model quadrangular (hexagonal) columnar basalt with cross-joints by specifying that two (three) of the ubiquitous joints are oriented along the column axis, and another along the cross-joints. The model elastic behavior accounts for the compliance of the column matrix and that of the joints. The criterion for failure on the planes con

property_groups.name: Properties
property_groups.description: Material properties for the 'columnar-basalt' zone constitutive model. Assign the model with 'zone cmodel assign columnar-basalt [range ...]', then set these with 'zone property <keyword> <value> [range cmodel-name columnar-basalt]'.

properties.description: matrix cohesion, c = c_1.
matrix dilation angle, \psi = \psi_1. The default is 0.0.
dip angle [degrees] of weakness plane of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
dip direction [degrees] of weakness plane of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
matrix friction angle, \phi = \phi_1.
joint cohesion, c_j of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
joint dilation angle, \psi_j of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set. The default is 0.0.
joint friction angle of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set, \phi_j.
joint tension limit, \sigma^t_j of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set. The default is 0.0.
x-component of reference unit vector for amplification.
y-component of reference unit vector for amplification.
z-component of reference unit vector for amplification.
x-component of the normal direction to the weakness plane, n_x, of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
y-component of the normal direction to the weakness plane, n_y of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
z-component of the normal direction to the weakness plane, n_z of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
Poisson ratio for the column matrix.

Space for joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set, S_i. S_i = 0 (default value) means joint i (1,2,3, or 4) is inactive. S_i > 0 means the joint is active for detection of shear and tensile yielding. However, it may not contribute to the stiffness matrix, if k_{n,i} = k_{n,i} = 0.
Normal stiffness of joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set, k_{n,i}.
Shear stiffness of joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set, k_{s,i}.

Amplification table for joint dilation 1, 2, 3. It should provide entries between 0 and 90 degree. Dilation amplification factor affects joint 1 to 3. Presumably, the cross joint is not affected by dilation amplification, thus if present, it should be declared as joint 4.
tension limit, {\sigma}^t. The default is 0.0.
Young’s modulus for the column matrix.
If true, the matrix plasticity will be considered, otherwise the matrix is elastic. The default is true.
Creep parameter A of joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set. Valid only when creep is configured.
Creep parameter n of joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set. Valid only when creep is configured.
Creep parameter s_{lim} of joint of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set. Valid only when creep is configured.
name of the table relating joint cohesion c_j = c_{j1} to joint plastic shear strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
name of the table relating joint dilation \psi_j = \psi_{j1} to joint plastic shear strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
name of the table relating joint friction angle \phi_j = \phi_{j1} to joint plastic shear strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
name of the table relating joint tension limit \sigma^t_j to joint plastic tensile strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
accumulated joint plastic shear strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
accumulated joint plastic tensile strain of the i-th (i=1,2,3,or 4) set.
